task: Extract structured atomic knowledge cards from a source page for a retrieval-augmented Tibetan medicine QA system.
Each card should describe one self-contained knowledge unit that can be retrieved independently.
requirements: Only extract information explicitly supported by the provided context.
Do not add external knowledge, modern clinical advice, or unsupported interpretation.
Split dense paragraphs into multiple atomic cards when they contain separate entities, symptoms, medicines, treatments, diagnostics, contraindications, or theory points.
Merge repeated statements on the same page only when they express the same fact.
Return JSON Lines only, one complete JSON object per line.
Use card_type='fact' for factual attributes and card_type='case' for condition-to-conclusion reasoning cases.
Use card_type='fact' for definitions, properties, functions, indications, contraindications, diagnostic signs, treatment methods, and theoretical claims.
Use card_type='case' when the context describes a symptom/sign pattern that supports a diagnosis, syndrome, treatment choice, or conclusion.
The content field must be concise, complete, and understandable without reading the original page.
The title field should be short and retrieval-friendly.
The entity field should contain the main disease, medicine, formula, symptom, diagnostic method, treatment, or theory concept.

The structured_fields object may include keys such as category, properties, functions, indications, contraindications, symptoms, diagnosis, treatment, pulse_sign, urine_sign, dosage, preparation, source_relation, or reasoning_basis when supported.
The retrieval_enhancement.keywords list should include synonyms, related terms, and short retrieval phrases that appear in or are directly implied by the context.
The evidence.citation_text field should copy a short source-supported excerpt from the page.
Keep card_id stable and unique using the provided book abbreviation, page number, and a three-digit sequence number.
Return [] if the context does not contain extractable domain knowledge.
Return no Markdown, no comments, and no explanatory text outside JSON Lines.

schema.title: short card title
schema.content: natural-language card content supported by the context

quality_checks: A card is valid only if its content can be verified from evidence.citation_text and the page context.
Avoid vague cards such as 'this passage discusses treatment'.
Avoid cards that combine unrelated knowledge points.
Avoid hallucinated book titles, page numbers, card IDs, or citations.

output_example.title: example entity diagnostic sign
output_example.content: A concise source-supported statement extracted from the page.
